sections.paragraph:
θεράπων, οντος, ὁ (s. two prec. entries; Hom.+; Alciphron 4, 19, 9 Διονύσου θ. καὶ προφήτης; SIG 219, 12; 1168, 114f οἱ θεράποντες of the temple of Asclepius; BGU 361 III, 18; LXX [Thackeray p. 7f]; TestSol, TestJob; TestLevi 4:2; ApcMos prol.; Philo) one who renders devoted service, esp. as an attendant in a cultic setting, attendant, aide, servant, servant in our lit. only of Moses (as Ex 4:10; 14:31; Num 12:7 [in Just., D. 46, 3 al.]; Wsd 10:16; Theoph. Ant. 3, 18 [p. 240, 13]) Hb 3:5; 1 Cl 4:12; 43:1; 51:3; cp. vs. 5; 53:5; B 14:4.—B. 1334. DELG. M-M. TW. Sv.

prec. prec. = preceding

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Thackeray Thackeray = HT., A Grammar of the OT in Greek according to the Septuagint—List 6

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

TestLevi TestLevi = Testament of Levi, s. Test12Patr—List 2

ApcMos ApcMos = Apocalypse of Moses—List 2

prol. prol. = prologus (prolog, prologue)

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

esp. esp. = especially

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Theoph. Ant. Theoph. Ant. = Theophilus Antiochenus, II a.d.—List 5

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 453
